name: Jon Ossoff
subject: Yesterday's rally in Atlanta
disclaimer_text: paid for by secret donors who banked on both parties
body:
#

Yesterday I rallied a crowd in Atlanta to oppose this Administration’s unprecedented attack on our constitution and the rule of law.
[](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-feb26.GJoCgS&refcode=em260208-1-f&t=1&akid=1600%2E5691347%2ESfMo5I)
[(You can watch my speech here on YouTube.)](https://act.electjon.com/go/3932?t=2&akid=1600%2E5691347%2ESfMo5I)
Here’s what I said:
Now, Atlanta, this election is nine months away, but the crowds I've seen over the last year at rallies like this one, the crowds every week in people's homes and backyards across our state, as we organize and mobilize toward victory, leave no doubt that Georgia is ready to do our part to save Our Republic again.
Many of you are here because you just can't stand what's being done to our country. You're seeing what I'm seeing, right? The President is posting about the Obamas like a Klansman.
You see our government transformed into a tool of one man's personal vengeance and power and enrichment.
You see the president and his family raking in billions while Americans struggle to make ends meet.
You see prices going up while jobs are getting harder to find.
You see millions lose insurance and local hospitals shut services while the rich get tax cuts.
You see masked federal agents kicking down doors without so much as a warrant from a judge, dragging Americans without cause, demanding papers at police checkpoints, assaulting and even killing Americans with impunity.
From the very top, the American government immediately slanders the citizens it kills as terrorists. You see prison camps growing full of children.
More and more of your neighbors carry their passports to the store in case they're detained based on their accent or the color of their skin.
The Justice Department and now even our intelligence agencies are openly politicized and turned against the president's enemies.
It feels like every day, the depravity deepens, the chaos accelerates.
Maybe you fear whether our Republic now, in our 250th year, can survive. But Atlanta, I'm here to tell you, we will overcome this together.
Some people just happen to live at times and in places where history is made. In Atlanta this year, once again, that's us, and it's not the first time the whole country is counting on us.
_So, are you ready to fight? Are you ready to win?_
Maybe you’ve felt surrounded by darkness this last year, but the first light of dawn is on the horizon.
Look at Texas. Look at Virginia. Look at New Jersey.
Look here in Georgia, where Democrats just won two statewide races by 25 points.
Look at Minneapolis, where they organized to protect children from being kidnapped at school, where tens of thousands marched in protest, where non-violent civil disobedience forced the tyrant to retreat.
And come November, the people who have been looting this country and desecrating our values are going to feel it.
And here’s the thing: They know it. And they fear it. And that’s why their abuses are becoming more reckless and desperate.
But as long as we don't give in to doubt or despair or division, our victories will be so decisive that no half baked plot hatched in the White House will foil the will of the people.
The power of the Presidency is nothing compared to the power of the American people.
_Are you ready to win?_
Every day our wave grows taller and our tent grows bigger. All across Georgia, more and more people of every background and belief reject the chaos and corruption.
It's not just Democrats and independents. Republicans take me aside, or they call me on the phone.
They tell me they're afraid of where this President has taken us, and they don't want it to go on.
They tell me they're voting for a Democrat for the first time.
And it's no mystery why. This is the cruelest and most arrogant group of people who have ever ascended to high offices, and their cruelty and their arrogance are matched only by their incompetence.
The President said he brings down prices on day one, instead, his tariffs keep driving prices higher and higher.
Coffee is up 20%. Beef and the power bill, both up 13%. Apples up 10%. I could go on, all in one year.
Unemployment is rising too. Listen to this.
American manufacturing jobs have shrunk nearly every single month since Trump rolled out his tariffs. Last month, more Americans lost their jobs than any January in 17 years.
And while prices are going up and jobs are getting harder to find, they decided to let health insurance premiums double for more than 20 million Americans, including more than a million Georgians.
200,000 Georgians have already lost their insurance.
I heard from a woman in her early 60s who waits tables for a living. She's fighting cancer. She told me she was going to lose her health coverage in the middle of chemotherapy.
Now you remember we were told that MAGA was for the working class in America.
But this is a government of, by, and for the ultra-rich. It’s the richest cabinet ever. It’s the Epstein class.
They are all the elites they pretend to hate.
The prices are up, jobs are going away, Medicaid and school lunches are slashed. Nursing homes are getting defunded.
If you're Steve Bannon, and your pitch was "Trump: for the forgotten man and woman," how do you sell any of this?
Trump was supposed to fight for the working class. Instead, he's literally closing rural clinics and hospitals to cut taxes for George Soros and Elon Musk.
He was supposed to end the globalist world-police foreign policy. Instead we're doing war for oil and nation-building again, and threatening to conquer Greenland.
He was supposed to drain the swamp. Instead, this is the most corrupt administration of all time, and everybody knows it.
Here's what Marjorie Taylor Greene said about MAGA just this week: Marjorie said, "I think people are realizing it was all a lie. What MAGA is really serving in this administration, who they're serving, is their big donors."
Guys, this is Marjorie Taylor Greene.
She said, "The big, big donors that donated all the money and continue to donate to the President's PACs and are donating to the big ballroom, those are the people who get the special favors. They get the government contracts, they get the pardons."
_Marjorie, welcome to the resistance._
I think never in the history of corruption has so little effort gone into hiding so much graft.
There’s the pardon factory they’re running out of DOJ — donors and political allies straight to the front of the line.
And then, of course, the President and his family's net worth has increased by billions since he took office.
The President and his sons have been very busy taking money from foreign governments and foreign interests.
There’s Sheikh Tahnoon, national security advisor in the United Arab Emirates, who deposited two billion dollars in Trump’s crypto firm just after the administration granted his government access to the most sensitive American AI chips.
There’s Justin Sun, the Chinese crypto titan under investigation by the SEC, who bought tens of millions of dollars of Trump coins just before his SEC investigation went away.
Dar Global, the real estate fund tied to Saudi royals, has new Trump deals in four countries, and the Governments of Qatar and Oman are investing, too.
But Trump doesn’t just want money from foreign governments. He wants money from the U.S. government, too! **So our President is suing the United States for $10 billion dollars.**
This is actually a thing he's doing. Did you see this? And if you're thinking, wait a minute, isn't that our tax dollars?
_The answer is yes, it is our tax dollars._
Of course, the President isn't just looking out for himself.
He's helping his friends and allies. Like: have you ever heard of a drug lord named Juan Orlando Hernandez?
It's a wild story, because he used to be the president of Honduras.
But a couple years ago, a federal judge here in the United States sentenced him to 45 years for conspiring to traffic hundreds of tons of cocaine into the United States.
He had taken millions from the Sinaloa Cartel and used the Honduran army and police to help them run drugs to America.
And then Donald Trump pardoned a man who helped the cartels make billions of dollars trafficking vast amounts of cocaine into the United States.
Donald Trump freed him because he was Donald Trump's political ally, and because this guy sent a four page letter to Trump saying he was a better president than Biden.
But then they will tell you with a straight face that the raid in Venezuela was about drugs.
See, Maduro should have just sent Trump a check or sponsored the ballroom.
By the way, Maduro's successor in Venezuela seems to have directed half a million to Trump's inauguration. Someone should probably look into that.
**The fact is, it pays to be friends with the President._It pays to kiss this President's ass._**
If you really need something, you can always just pay the President, whether you're a sheik or a felon or a kingpin or a political donor, there's a buffet of corrupt options to curry favor with this administration.
And yes, Donald Trump cut our health care so people like Elon Musk would pay lower taxes.
But don't forget, he also cut our health care to fund the creation of a huge federal paramilitary force.
Now look, there are still plenty of patriots serving our country in the U.S. government, and decent, hard working folks in federal law enforcement trying to do the right thing, but this ain't that.
And the American people are asking: _Why are roving gangs of masked men who looked like they couldn't pass the army physical exam, dressed up like 10 Delta Force operators wearing camouflage and carrying ARs on the streets of American cities, setting up checkpoints to harass commuters, demanding papers, dragging people screaming from their cars and shooting people to death?_
They don't have to identify themselves. They don't have to explain why you've been stopped. Film them, and they tell you you've been added to a terrorist watch list.
A Republican congressman running for Senate in Texas said, "When a federal officer gives you instructions, you abide by them and you get to keep your life."
Obey the federal government. Do what they tell us or die?
_What happened to don't tread on me?_ That if you're carrying a firearm, the Trump administration says they have license to kill you.
Are there any principled constitutional conservatives left in the United States? Because it's not just the Department of Homeland Security that’s shredding our Bill of Rights.
The administration is corrupting the Justice Department and even our intelligence agencies, and yes, they are laying the groundwork for mischief in the upcoming elections.
The President said we never need to vote again. Then he said we shouldn't have elections. Now, he says he wants to take over the elections.
And like a scene out of some banana republic, Tulsi Gabbard, the country’s spy chief, comes to Fulton County, Georgia to oversee the seizure of ballots.
Tulsi said the President asked her to go, which means the President is personally managing federal raids on election sites in battleground states, all in service of his obsession with overturning the 2020 election and laying the groundwork for whatever they're plotting this year.
But they made a big mistake. They came to Fulton County, Georgia.
They came to the political and spiritual heart of the civil rights movement.
They came to the doorstep of John Lewis’ congressional district.
And as a result, we are going to mobilize the biggest and most unstoppable turnout in state history.
Look, defeating Donald Trump and his allies is essential, but it's only part of the job ahead, because Donald Trump is a symptom of a deeper disease.
Decades of deepening political corruption, growing inequality of power and wealth — these are the failures of an ancient and visionless political class entrenched in a system built to keep them in office forever.
Members of Congress who just sit idly in their seats, collecting lobbyist checks, trading stocks, saying whatever the polls tell them to.
And on their watch, the national spirit that defeated fascism, that landed men on the moon, that passed civil rights laws, was replaced by a small and selfish politics.
That’s the politics of legalized corruption, defined by corporate PACs and bundlers and lobbyists.
It's a system designed to serve donors and protect companies, and then Citizens United came along with the worst court decision in modern American history, flooding the airwaves with ads paid for by secret donors who banked on both parties.
All of this bent Congress to serve the powerful and forget the people.
And that's why you still pay a fortune for prescriptions.
That's why your insurance company gets to keep an eye on your claim.
That's why hedge funds get to buy up all the houses in your neighborhood.
That's why that ambulance costs $3,000 after you just had to get a choking toddler to the hospital.
Our democracy stopped serving the public, and the public lost faith in a system that was failing them.
But it doesn't have to be this way. It doesn't have to be this way.
Everywhere I go, across our state, people tell me they are desperate for change and for new ideas.
The South Georgia farmer whose costs rise and earnings fall each year, whose kids have left the family farm, who's still waiting for federal aid long after the storm.
The student working through college who still needs food stamps to make rent — whose conscience can't stand their tax dollars being used to lock up immigrant children here at home or to kill innocent children in the Middle East.
The young couple expecting their first child — who worry how they’ll afford a crib and a stroller, let alone a first home — and their health insurance just went up a thousand dollars a per month.
There are so many problems we should be solving together. Guaranteeing all Americans great health insurance and great health care no matter our wealth, unleashing an affordable housing boom, saving our family farms, making America the world's human rights figure, it should excite us.
But none of it will be possible if we allow ourselves to forget what America stands for.
See Donald Trump, JD Vance, Stephen Miller, they offer a small, closed, menacing vision of America defined by fear and scarcity and exclusion.
They imitate the blood and soil rhetoric of history's worst regimes.
Immigrants, Donald Trump says, are "poisoning the blood of our country."
Those are the kinds of words used by the most dangerous men in history.
I listened to a speech a few months back by a Senator from Missouri aligned with the President. And what I think I heard him say was that the only real Americans are those descended from the original European settlers.
Now, maybe he forgot the Mayflower itself was full of religious exiles fleeing persecution.
But our heritage isn't limited to that of the pilgrims and those who settled the west.
America's heritage includes the descendants of slaves who won liberation from slavery Georgia in the 11th century.
It includes people from every region and continent who arrived here fleeing persecution or seeking opportunity.
Americans are not a race. We're a people united, not by ethnicity, but by shared convictions, and that is what makes us exceptional.
That the most powerful nation on earth proclaims as its core values that all are created equal with inalienable rights to life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness, no matter how humble our communities, no matter our ancestry, no matter when we arrived on these shores.
George Washington wrote in 1783, "America is open to receive not only the opulent and respectable stranger, but the oppressed and persecuted of all nations and religions."
_That’s a legacy worth protecting._
And I was thinking the other day, what would the founders think if they visited us today?
Well, they would see that slavery had been abolished.
They would see that Americans without land, and then women and then the descendants of slaves, had secured voting rights.
That American leadership in science and discovery had propelled knowledge and technology to unimaginable heights.
They would see that the United States had become the superpower that defeated Nazism and communism.
They would see a pluralistic democracy, people of all colors descended from every point on Earth, stitched together out of many nations into one still the greatest hope for mankind.
They will also learn that of the world's great rich nations, American life expectancy is the lowest, while our childhood poverty, murder and maternal mortality rates are the highest.
They would see a nation that at the height of its wealth and power fell into the very traps they most feared, the dominance of special interests, bitter division between warring political parties and a faithless President exploiting that rot to pursue the absolute power they overthrew.
But they gave us the tools to fix this. They put the power in our hands.
It's up to us, for the United States history is calling the question, _what kind of a people are we?_
I believe the measure of our success is not how many of the poorest and most vulnerable families we hunt down and shackle and imprison and deport.
The measure of our success is whether each generation achieves greater health and prosperity than the last and whether we live up to the profits for all.
I believe we can lift American children out of poverty.
We can keep our communities safe from violence.
We can make sure every mom and every baby in America gets the best care in the world.
I believe we can reform our corrupt politics.
We can unite the American people to solve big problems and achieve great things again.
I believe we will escape this moral tailspin and recommit ourselves to the values that have powered 250 years of problems.
But none of this is possible without victory this November, and the battle is already on.
_So Atlanta, are you ready to work? Are you ready to save the country again?_
Thank you so much, Atlanta.
* * *

_He is MAGA’s #1 target in the 2026 midterms._

clean_body: Yesterday I rallied a crowd in Atlanta to oppose this Administration’s unprecedented attack on our constitution and the rule of law. Here’s what I said: Now, Atlanta, this election is nine months away, but the crowds I've seen over the last year at rallies like this one, the crowds every week in people's homes and backyards across our state, as we organize and mobilize toward victory, leave no doubt that Georgia is ready to do our part to save Our Republic again. Many of you are here because you just can't stand what's being done to our country. You're seeing what I'm seeing, right? The President is posting about the Obamas like a Klansman. You see our government transformed into a tool of one man's personal vengeance and power and enrichment. You see the president and his family raking in billions while Americans struggle to make ends meet. You see prices going up while jobs are getting harder to find. You see millions lose insurance and local hospitals shut services while the rich get tax cuts. You see masked federal agents kicking down doors without so much as a warrant from a judge, dragging Americans without cause, demanding papers at police checkpoints, assaulting and even killing Americans with impunity. From the very top, the American government immediately slanders the citizens it kills as terrorists. You see prison camps growing full of children. More and more of your neighbors carry their passports to the store in case they're detained based on their accent or the color of their skin. The Justice Department and now even our intelligence agencies are openly politicized and turned against the president's enemies. It feels like every day, the depravity deepens, the chaos accelerates. Maybe you fear whether our Republic now, in our 250th year, can survive. But Atlanta, I'm here to tell you, we will overcome this together. Some people just happen to live at times and in places where history is made. In Atlanta this year, once again, that's us, and it's not the first time the whole country is counting on us. So, are you ready to fight? Are you ready to win? Maybe you’ve felt surrounded by darkness this last year, but the first light of dawn is on the horizon. Look at Texas. Look at Virginia. Look at New Jersey. Look here in Georgia, where Democrats just won two statewide races by 25 points. Look at Minneapolis, where they organized to protect children from being kidnapped at school, where tens of thousands marched in protest, where non-violent civil disobedience forced the tyrant to retreat. And come November, the people who have been looting this country and desecrating our values are going to feel it. And here’s the thing: They know it. And they fear it. And that’s why their abuses are becoming more reckless and desperate. But as long as we don't give in to doubt or despair or division, our victories will be so decisive that no half baked plot hatched in the White House will foil the will of the people. The power of the Presidency is nothing compared to the power of the American people. Are you ready to win? Every day our wave grows taller and our tent grows bigger. All across Georgia, more and more people of every background and belief reject the chaos and corruption. It's not just Democrats and independents. Republicans take me aside, or they call me on the phone. They tell me they're afraid of where this President has taken us, and they don't want it to go on. They tell me they're voting for a Democrat for the first time. And it's no mystery why. This is the cruelest and most arrogant group of people who have ever ascended to high offices, and their cruelty and their arrogance are matched only by their incompetence. The President said he brings down prices on day one, instead, his tariffs keep driving prices higher and higher. Coffee is up 20%. Beef and the power bill, both up 13%. Apples up 10%. I could go on, all in one year. Unemployment is rising too. Listen to this. American manufacturing jobs have shrunk nearly every single month since Trump rolled out his tariffs. Last month, more Americans lost their jobs than any January in 17 years. And while prices are going up and jobs are getting harder to find, they decided to let health insurance premiums double for more than 20 million Americans, including more than a million Georgians. 200,000 Georgians have already lost their insurance. I heard from a woman in her early 60s who waits tables for a living. She's fighting cancer. She told me she was going to lose her health coverage in the middle of chemotherapy. Now you remember we were told that MAGA was for the working class in America. But this is a government of, by, and for the ultra-rich. It’s the richest cabinet ever. It’s the Epstein class. They are all the elites they pretend to hate. The prices are up, jobs are going away, Medicaid and school lunches are slashed. Nursing homes are getting defunded. If you're Steve Bannon, and your pitch was "Trump: for the forgotten man and woman," how do you sell any of this? Trump was supposed to fight for the working class. Instead, he's literally closing rural clinics and hospitals to cut taxes for George Soros and Elon Musk. He was supposed to end the globalist world-police foreign policy. Instead we're doing war for oil and nation-building again, and threatening to conquer Greenland. He was supposed to drain the swamp. Instead, this is the most corrupt administration of all time, and everybody knows it. Here's what Marjorie Taylor Greene said about MAGA just this week: Marjorie said, "I think people are realizing it was all a lie. What MAGA is really serving in this administration, who they're serving, is their big donors." Guys, this is Marjorie Taylor Greene. She said, "The big, big donors that donated all the money and continue to donate to the President's PACs and are donating to the big ballroom, those are the people who get the special favors. They get the government contracts, they get the pardons." Marjorie, welcome to the resistance. I think never in the history of corruption has so little effort gone into hiding so much graft. There’s the pardon factory they’re running out of DOJ — donors and political allies straight to the front of the line. And then, of course, the President and his family's net worth has increased by billions since he took office. The President and his sons have been very busy taking money from foreign governments and foreign interests. There’s Sheikh Tahnoon, national security advisor in the United Arab Emirates, who deposited two billion dollars in Trump’s crypto firm just after the administration granted his government access to the most sensitive American AI chips. There’s Justin Sun, the Chinese crypto titan under investigation by the SEC, who bought tens of millions of dollars of Trump coins just before his SEC investigation went away. Dar Global, the real estate fund tied to Saudi royals, has new Trump deals in four countries, and the Governments of Qatar and Oman are investing, too. But Trump doesn’t just want money from foreign governments. He wants money from the U.S. government, too! So our President is suing the United States for $10 billion dollars. This is actually a thing he's doing. Did you see this? And if you're thinking, wait a minute, isn't that our tax dollars? The answer is yes, it is our tax dollars. Of course, the President isn't just looking out for himself. He's helping his friends and allies. Like: have you ever heard of a drug lord named Juan Orlando Hernandez? It's a wild story, because he used to be the president of Honduras. But a couple years ago, a federal judge here in the United States sentenced him to 45 years for conspiring to traffic hundreds of tons of cocaine into the United States. He had taken millions from the Sinaloa Cartel and used the Honduran army and police to help them run drugs to America. And then Donald Trump pardoned a man who helped the cartels make billions of dollars trafficking vast amounts of cocaine into the United States. Donald Trump freed him because he was Donald Trump's political ally, and because this guy sent a four page letter to Trump saying he was a better president than Biden. But then they will tell you with a straight face that the raid in Venezuela was about drugs. See, Maduro should have just sent Trump a check or sponsored the ballroom. By the way, Maduro's successor in Venezuela seems to have directed half a million to Trump's inauguration. Someone should probably look into that. The fact is, it pays to be friends with the President.It pays to kiss this President's ass. If you really need something, you can always just pay the President, whether you're a sheik or a felon or a kingpin or a political donor, there's a buffet of corrupt options to curry favor with this administration. And yes, Donald Trump cut our health care so people like Elon Musk would pay lower taxes. But don't forget, he also cut our health care to fund the creation of a huge federal paramilitary force. Now look, there are still plenty of patriots serving our country in the U.S. government, and decent, hard working folks in federal law enforcement trying to do the right thing, but this ain't that. And the American people are asking: Why are roving gangs of masked men who looked like they couldn't pass the army physical exam, dressed up like 10 Delta Force operators wearing camouflage and carrying ARs on the streets of American cities, setting up checkpoints to harass commuters, demanding papers, dragging people screaming from their cars and shooting people to death? They don't have to identify themselves. They don't have to explain why you've been stopped. Film them, and they tell you you've been added to a terrorist watch list. A Republican congressman running for Senate in Texas said, "When a federal officer gives you instructions, you abide by them and you get to keep your life." Obey the federal government. Do what they tell us or die? What happened to don't tread on me? That if you're carrying a firearm, the Trump administration says they have license to kill you. Are there any principled constitutional conservatives left in the United States? Because it's not just the Department of Homeland Security that’s shredding our Bill of Rights. The administration is corrupting the Justice Department and even our intelligence agencies, and yes, they are laying the groundwork for mischief in the upcoming elections. The President said we never need to vote again. Then he said we shouldn't have elections. Now, he says he wants to take over the elections. And like a scene out of some banana republic, Tulsi Gabbard, the country’s spy chief, comes to Fulton County, Georgia to oversee the seizure of ballots. Tulsi said the President asked her to go, which means the President is personally managing federal raids on election sites in battleground states, all in service of his obsession with overturning the 2020 election and laying the groundwork for whatever they're plotting this year. But they made a big mistake. They came to Fulton County, Georgia. They came to the political and spiritual heart of the civil rights movement. They came to the doorstep of John Lewis’ congressional district. And as a result, we are going to mobilize the biggest and most unstoppable turnout in state history. Look, defeating Donald Trump and his allies is essential, but it's only part of the job ahead, because Donald Trump is a symptom of a deeper disease. Decades of deepening political corruption, growing inequality of power and wealth — these are the failures of an ancient and visionless political class entrenched in a system built to keep them in office forever. Members of Congress who just sit idly in their seats, collecting lobbyist checks, trading stocks, saying whatever the polls tell them to. And on their watch, the national spirit that defeated fascism, that landed men on the moon, that passed civil rights laws, was replaced by a small and selfish politics. That’s the politics of legalized corruption, defined by corporate PACs and bundlers and lobbyists. It's a system designed to serve donors and protect companies, and then Citizens United came along with the worst court decision in modern American history, flooding the airwaves with ads paid for by secret donors who banked on both parties. All of this bent Congress to serve the powerful and forget the people. And that's why you still pay a fortune for prescriptions. That's why your insurance company gets to keep an eye on your claim. That's why hedge funds get to buy up all the houses in your neighborhood. That's why that ambulance costs $3,000 after you just had to get a choking toddler to the hospital. Our democracy stopped serving the public, and the public lost faith in a system that was failing them. But it doesn't have to be this way. It doesn't have to be this way. Everywhere I go, across our state, people tell me they are desperate for change and for new ideas. The South Georgia farmer whose costs rise and earnings fall each year, whose kids have left the family farm, who's still waiting for federal aid long after the storm. The student working through college who still needs food stamps to make rent — whose conscience can't stand their tax dollars being used to lock up immigrant children here at home or to kill innocent children in the Middle East. The young couple expecting their first child — who worry how they’ll afford a crib and a stroller, let alone a first home — and their health insurance just went up a thousand dollars a per month. There are so many problems we should be solving together. Guaranteeing all Americans great health insurance and great health care no matter our wealth, unleashing an affordable housing boom, saving our family farms, making America the world's human rights figure, it should excite us. But none of it will be possible if we allow ourselves to forget what America stands for. See Donald Trump, JD Vance, Stephen Miller, they offer a small, closed, menacing vision of America defined by fear and scarcity and exclusion. They imitate the blood and soil rhetoric of history's worst regimes. Immigrants, Donald Trump says, are "poisoning the blood of our country." Those are the kinds of words used by the most dangerous men in history. I listened to a speech a few months back by a Senator from Missouri aligned with the President. And what I think I heard him say was that the only real Americans are those descended from the original European settlers. Now, maybe he forgot the Mayflower itself was full of religious exiles fleeing persecution. But our heritage isn't limited to that of the pilgrims and those who settled the west. America's heritage includes the descendants of slaves who won liberation from slavery Georgia in the 11th century. It includes people from every region and continent who arrived here fleeing persecution or seeking opportunity. Americans are not a race. We're a people united, not by ethnicity, but by shared convictions, and that is what makes us exceptional. That the most powerful nation on earth proclaims as its core values that all are created equal with inalienable rights to life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness, no matter how humble our communities, no matter our ancestry, no matter when we arrived on these shores. George Washington wrote in 1783, "America is open to receive not only the opulent and respectable stranger, but the oppressed and persecuted of all nations and religions." That’s a legacy worth protecting. And I was thinking the other day, what would the founders think if they visited us today? Well, they would see that slavery had been abolished. They would see that Americans without land, and then women and then the descendants of slaves, had secured voting rights. That American leadership in science and discovery had propelled knowledge and technology to unimaginable heights. They would see that the United States had become the superpower that defeated Nazism and communism. They would see a pluralistic democracy, people of all colors descended from every point on Earth, stitched together out of many nations into one still the greatest hope for mankind. They will also learn that of the world's great rich nations, American life expectancy is the lowest, while our childhood poverty, murder and maternal mortality rates are the highest. They would see a nation that at the height of its wealth and power fell into the very traps they most feared, the dominance of special interests, bitter division between warring political parties and a faithless President exploiting that rot to pursue the absolute power they overthrew. But they gave us the tools to fix this. They put the power in our hands. It's up to us, for the United States history is calling the question, what kind of a people are we? I believe the measure of our success is not how many of the poorest and most vulnerable families we hunt down and shackle and imprison and deport. The measure of our success is whether each generation achieves greater health and prosperity than the last and whether we live up to the profits for all. I believe we can lift American children out of poverty. We can keep our communities safe from violence. We can make sure every mom and every baby in America gets the best care in the world. I believe we can reform our corrupt politics. We can unite the American people to solve big problems and achieve great things again. I believe we will escape this moral tailspin and recommit ourselves to the values that have powered 250 years of problems. But none of this is possible without victory this November, and the battle is already on. So Atlanta, are you ready to work? Are you ready to save the country again? Thank you so much, Atlanta. 👋 Wait! Before you go... 👋 Jon Ossoff in Georgia is the ONLY Democratic Senator running for re-election in a state Trump won. He is MAGA’s #1 target in the 2026 midterms. ** Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate

null, do you know what’s happening in Michigan?

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: This deal is anything but Super
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=2765%2E4032634%2EosXYrz)
---
Happy Super Bowl Sunday. First and foremost, I want to say: Go Pats! While millions are gearing up to watch the big game, I want to talk about something that happened this past week that should make every football fan — every consumer — worried. The Trump administration just approved a billion-dollar acquisition deal that gives the NFL a 10% equity stake in ESPN in exchange for control over key streaming assets, including NFL Network, NFL RedZone, and NFL Fantasy operations. **Translation:** A massive consolidation that will mean higher costs, and fewer choices to watch games. ESPN and the NFL are selling this as a win for fans: More games, more coverage, more access. Sound familiar? It's the same playbook corporations always use: More! Better! Bigger! But that’s not exactly what’s going on here. It's a massive consolidation of sports media power that was rushed through with almost no public scrutiny. And that raises some very real concerns:
* What happens when **the people running the game also own part of the channel** showing it?
* Who's looking out for consumers when **the league and the network are both trying to make money** off the same deal?
* How will prices be impacted when **one media company oversees all streaming options?**

These aren't hypotheticals. We've been sounding the alarm about these concerns long before this deal touched down. Look, football fans deserve good, healthy competition — both on and off the field. When one company can determine whatever price they want to watch a game, fans will have no choice but to pay up or just not watch. **We have antitrust laws in place to put a stop to this kind of corporate concentration — and I’m fighting to enforce them.** [**If you're sick and tired of media companies charging you more and more and more just to watch your favorite teams play, and if you're able to, please pitch in anything you can today to help fight back against corporate greed.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3456?t=8&akid=2765%2E4032634%2EosXYrz)

Peter,

Right now, families across the country are feeling the consequences of
political decisions made by Republicans. The balance of power in Congress
is hanging by a thread.

So now is the most important time to strengthen our movement.

The Squad is standing together to push back against the Trump
administration. They’re fighting for accountability, justice, and a
government that actually works for working people.

But the truth is that their bold leadership doesn’t go unchallenged.
Powerful interests are already working to silence and defeat our
progressive champions in Congress — Alexandria, Ayanna, Ilhan, and Rashida
— and the stakes couldn’t be higher.

This election cycle, we have four critical seats to defend. Losing even
one would weaken the fight for justice, equity, and real accountability in
Congress.

The Squad Victory Fund defends our leaders and supercharges grassroots
organizing to win tough races, show up for our communities, and build
progressive power that lasts beyond November. But we can’t do it without
you.

The Squad Victory Fund

Why Democratic "Guardrails" for DHS and ICE Are Moral Theater, Not Accountability

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.bO3DXt-RnlmVEZHyvIZp1Ug_bQZOZMq_6PI-JZnEfsk?) for more

# [You Can't Reform a Police State: Defund DHS and ICE Now](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=187151913&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg3MTUxOTEzLCJpYXQiOjE3NzA1NzQwOTEsImV4cCI6MTc3MzE2NjA5MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.0mUVpVFkthy5DLa2eqo7wPw3Y-lbKnDWJrIpHSfWokA)
### Why Democratic "Guardrails" for DHS and ICE Are Moral Theater, Not Accountability
| | [David Shuster](https://substack.com/@davidshuster) and [Blue Amp Media](https://substack.com/@blueamp)
---
| Feb 8
---
| [](https://substack.com/@davidshuster)| | [](https://substack.com/@blueamp)
---|---|---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=187151913&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg3MTUxOTEzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc3MDU3NDA5MSwiZXhwIjoxNzczMTY2MDkxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzcyMjUzMiIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.dPDi_zp-oIw0mF4XfFWhrYTbYYiZE_TXYflQGljmB5k&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=187151913&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg3MTUxOTEzLCJpYXQiOjE3NzA1NzQwOTEsImV4cCI6MTc3MzE2NjA5MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.0mUVpVFkthy5DLa2eqo7wPw3Y-lbKnDWJrIpHSfWokA&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=187151913&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg3MTUxOTEzLCJpYXQiOjE3NzA1NzQwOTEsImV4cCI6MTc3MzE2NjA5MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.0mUVpVFkthy5DLa2eqo7wPw3Y-lbKnDWJrIpHSfWokA)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvYmx1ZWFtcC9wL2Vub3VnaC1hbHJlYWR5LWRlZnVuZC1tZWFucy1kZWZ1bmRkZWZ1bmQ_.AhRshvHz2g_236fkEDp_hdqdsA9oMtrBjTAAVlzk6CY?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/blueamp/p/enough-already-defund-means-defunddefund?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a6a5a6d0-a82c-4a1e-b0ef-c928bf979ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a6a5a6d0-a82c-4a1e-b0ef-c928bf979ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Blue Amp Media is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.0vUYQt8M-4Ve6HEqieqmIfcMRurCYJ_aR1mwkiL-4Xc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=187151913)
[Buy us a coffee!](https://substack.com/redirect/9ec3c431-9de9-4bcd-a8d7-a17b9946770e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
_**By David Shuster**_
One of the most enduring superstitions in American politics is the belief that a bad institution can be made virtuous by scolding it sternly, announcing symbolic restrictions, and then increasing its allowance. This view, while touching in its innocence, is now on full display in the democratic congressional leaders attempt to reform the Trump administration's Department of Homeland Security and Immigration and Customs Enforcement Agency.
The theory goes like this: DHS and ICE have become lawless, brutal, and contemptuous of constitutional norms--but if Congress just insists loudly enough on "new guardrails," the agencies will suddenly develop consciences.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a6a5a6d0-a82c-4a1e-b0ef-c928bf979ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
One imagines a group of federal agents, already accustomed to masked raids, warrantless entries, and bureaucratic indifference to human suffering, pausing mid-operation to say, "Hold on, fellow proud boys, uh DHS squads... the appropriations language demands better vibes."
In the current DHS funding dispute, democrats have pushed for a list of reforms as conditions for voting to keep DHS open. The democrats, in a demand letter to congressional republicans, are proposing body cameras and agent identification requirements including the elimination of agent face masks and a prohibition on military style uniforms.
* * *
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.0vUYQt8M-4Ve6HEqieqmIfcMRurCYJ_aR1mwkiL-4Xc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=187151913)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a6a5a6d0-a82c-4a1e-b0ef-c928bf979ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The democrats also want restrictions on where agents can go and operate, plus promises that DHS will cooperate with state and local law enforcement investigations into federal agents who use unlawful force.
So, if an ICE agent shoots a protestor in the face and kills that person, federal agents may not destroy the evidence and flee the crime scene. Kristi Noem, do you pinky promise?
Many of these provisions already exist in federal law. And other requests are toothless. The body camera proposal, for example, is especially rich.
* * *
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=187151913&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg3MTUxOTEzLCJpYXQiOjE3NzA1NzQwOTEsImV4cCI6MTc3MzE2NjA5MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.0mUVpVFkthy5DLa2eqo7wPw3Y-lbKnDWJrIpHSfWokA)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a6a5a6d0-a82c-4a1e-b0ef-c928bf979ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Under that proposal, agents must be _issued_ cameras but are under no binding obligation to turn them on, keep them on, or face meaningful consequences when the footage disappears. Also, being on camera didn't stop federal agents from shooting and killing Renee Good and Alex Pretti.
Still, this is reform in the modern political public relations sense: a press release dressed up as a safeguard. The camera exists not to actually stop or restrain abuse, but to reassure democratic donors and grass roots activists that with democratic congressional leaders, Something Is Being Done.
Meanwhile the more fundamental and foundational rot at DHS is going unmentioned.
* * *
[Leave a comment](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=187151913&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg3MTUxOTEzLCJpYXQiOjE3NzA1NzQwOTEsImV4cCI6MTc3MzE2NjA5MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.0mUVpVFkthy5DLa2eqo7wPw3Y-lbKnDWJrIpHSfWokA&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a6a5a6d0-a82c-4a1e-b0ef-c928bf979ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
DHS was designed, in a panic, after the 9-11 terror attacks. It was an agency birthed by fear, imbued with haste, and pumped full of enthusiasm for authority.
Under the Trump administration, the latent DHS authoritarianism has simply been encouraged to stretch its legs. ICE in particular now operates less as a law-enforcement agency and more like uniformed fascist thugs, accountable primarily to themselves, Stephen Miller, and a right wing fantasy world that treats cruelty as proof of seriousness.
* * *
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.0vUYQt8M-4Ve6HEqieqmIfcMRurCYJ_aR1mwkiL-4Xc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=187151913)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a6a5a6d0-a82c-4a1e-b0ef-c928bf979ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Democrats loudly object to this situation, as they should. And yet, before any talks began with congressional republicans, democratic leaders had already whittled down demands and lowered the temperature.
Initially, democrats pledged to Defund ICE. Period. In the latest YouGov polling, voters approve defunding ICE and DHS by a margin of 47% to 41%. But as soon as Republican Senate leader Thune said "let's talk," democrats torpedoed the defunding rhetoric. What about defunding just ICE? The latest YouGov poll found 58% of Americans disapprove of ICE. But democrats, in their first demand letter sent to GOP leaders, dropped any mention of eliminating ICE and instead wrote about "reform."
So, while Democratic Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer and House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries publicly fret about the Trump administration breaking law enforcement norms, the democrats have already begun to back track and signal it won't take much to keep DHS and ICE going. The democrats have even dropped their demand that Trump fire DHS Secretary Kristi Noem.
* * *
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a6a5a6d0-a82c-4a1e-b0ef-c928bf979ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a6a5a6d0-a82c-4a1e-b0ef-c928bf979ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This is not courage; it is moral bookkeeping. It allows the democratic lawmakers to register DHS disapproval and soothe the progressive base while not offending conservative voters. It also ensures that the deportation trains keep running on time.
The plain truth--unfashionable but stubborn--is that DHS and ICE are not malfunctioning. They are functioning exactly as the Trump White House wants. These agencies are brutalizing migrant workers and citizens alike, terrifying democratic communities, and putting people with black and brown skin on notice that they are not wanted in Trump's America.
The only possible disappointment for Trump and his goons might be that the protesters responses have been peaceful.
* * *
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=187151913&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTg3MTUxOTEzLCJpYXQiOjE3NzA1NzQwOTEsImV4cCI6MTc3MzE2NjA5MSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.0mUVpVFkthy5DLa2eqo7wPw3Y-lbKnDWJrIpHSfWokA)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a6a5a6d0-a82c-4a1e-b0ef-c928bf979ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Instead of breaking glass and burning buildings - actions that would undoubtedly trigger a Trump military response faster than you can say "martial law" or "insurrection act," Minnesotans are marching, singing and blowing whistles. There have been no riots.
If the protesters in sub zero temperatures can meet federal abuse with fortitude, discipline, and peaceful protest, surely democratic leaders in Congress can show some political courage and backbone. If democrats genuinely believe, as they should, that DHS and ICE are lawless, abusive, and corrosive, then the remedy is obvious and unpleasant: defund them. Starve the agencies. Break them apart. Separate disaster relief and aviation security from immigration enforcement. And stop going along with the absurd notion that every federal function must be housed under one militarized Trump agency roof.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a6a5a6d0-a82c-4a1e-b0ef-c928bf979ed6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
If Democrats are ever going to produce meaningful change, we should be willing to take real, structural action and not settle for symbolic reforms. This isn't a time for democratic party modesty and pragmatism. People are being rounded up and disappeared. Protesters have been shot and killed. And DHS still refuses to fully cooperate with local Minneapolis prosecutors.
Defunding is not radical. It is the only language Donald Trump and his republican enablers in Congress will understand. Everything else is sermonizing--cheap, shallow, and utterly ineffective
Politically and morally, democrats must be bold and strong. It's not enough to express shock at Trump's DHS, request technology changes, and ask federal agents to be more respectful of the constitution.
It's time for democrats to defund DHS's enforcement arm and start over with new agencies and institutions that respect both the law and human rights.
Enough already.

Why Democratic "Guardrails" for DHS and ICE Are Moral Theater, Not Accountability | | and | Feb 8 | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- Blue Amp Media is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. By David Shuster One of the most enduring superstitions in American politics is the belief that a bad institution can be made virtuous by scolding it sternly, announcing symbolic restrictions, and then increasing its allowance. This view, while touching in its innocence, is now on full display in the democratic congressional leaders attempt to reform the Trump administration's Department of Homeland Security and Immigration and Customs Enforcement Agency. The theory goes like this: DHS and ICE have become lawless, brutal, and contemptuous of constitutional norms--but if Congress just insists loudly enough on "new guardrails," the agencies will suddenly develop consciences. | | ---|---|--- One imagines a group of federal agents, already accustomed to masked raids, warrantless entries, and bureaucratic indifference to human suffering, pausing mid-operation to say, "Hold on, fellow proud boys, uh DHS squads... the appropriations language demands better vibes." In the current DHS funding dispute, democrats have pushed for a list of reforms as conditions for voting to keep DHS open. The democrats, in a demand letter to congressional republicans, are proposing body cameras and agent identification requirements including the elimination of agent face masks and a prohibition on military style uniforms. | | ---|---|--- The democrats also want restrictions on where agents can go and operate, plus promises that DHS will cooperate with state and local law enforcement investigations into federal agents who use unlawful force. So, if an ICE agent shoots a protestor in the face and kills that person, federal agents may not destroy the evidence and flee the crime scene. Kristi Noem, do you pinky promise? Many of these provisions already exist in federal law. And other requests are toothless. The body camera proposal, for example, is especially rich. | | ---|---|--- Under that proposal, agents must be issued cameras but are under no binding obligation to turn them on, keep them on, or face meaningful consequences when the footage disappears. Also, being on camera didn't stop federal agents from shooting and killing Renee Good and Alex Pretti. Still, this is reform in the modern political public relations sense: a press release dressed up as a safeguard. The camera exists not to actually stop or restrain abuse, but to reassure democratic donors and grass roots activists that with democratic congressional leaders, Something Is Being Done. Meanwhile the more fundamental and foundational rot at DHS is going unmentioned. | | ---|---|--- DHS was designed, in a panic, after the 9-11 terror attacks. It was an agency birthed by fear, imbued with haste, and pumped full of enthusiasm for authority. Under the Trump administration, the latent DHS authoritarianism has simply been encouraged to stretch its legs. ICE in particular now operates less as a law-enforcement agency and more like uniformed fascist thugs, accountable primarily to themselves, Stephen Miller, and a right wing fantasy world that treats cruelty as proof of seriousness. | | ---|---|--- Democrats loudly object to this situation, as they should. And yet, before any talks began with congressional republicans, democratic leaders had already whittled down demands and lowered the temperature. Initially, democrats pledged to Defund ICE. Period. In the latest YouGov polling, voters approve defunding ICE and DHS by a margin of 47% to 41%. But as soon as Republican Senate leader Thune said "let's talk," democrats torpedoed the defunding rhetoric. What about defunding just ICE? The latest YouGov poll found 58% of Americans disapprove of ICE. But democrats, in their first demand letter sent to GOP leaders, dropped any mention of eliminating ICE and instead wrote about "reform." So, while Democratic Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer and House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries publicly fret about the Trump administration breaking law enforcement norms, the democrats have already begun to back track and signal it won't take much to keep DHS and ICE going. The democrats have even dropped their demand that Trump fire DHS Secretary Kristi Noem. | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- This is not courage; it is moral bookkeeping. It allows the democratic lawmakers to register DHS disapproval and soothe the progressive base while not offending conservative voters. It also ensures that the deportation trains keep running on time. The plain truth--unfashionable but stubborn--is that DHS and ICE are not malfunctioning. They are functioning exactly as the Trump White House wants. These agencies are brutalizing migrant workers and citizens alike, terrifying democratic communities, and putting people with black and brown skin on notice that they are not wanted in Trump's America. The only possible disappointment for Trump and his goons might be that the protesters responses have been peaceful. | | ---|---|--- Instead of breaking glass and burning buildings - actions that would undoubtedly trigger a Trump military response faster than you can say "martial law" or "insurrection act," Minnesotans are marching, singing and blowing whistles. There have been no riots. If the protesters in sub zero temperatures can meet federal abuse with fortitude, discipline, and peaceful protest, surely democratic leaders in Congress can show some political courage and backbone. If democrats genuinely believe, as they should, that DHS and ICE are lawless, abusive, and corrosive, then the remedy is obvious and unpleasant: defund them. Starve the agencies. Break them apart. Separate disaster relief and aviation security from immigration enforcement. And stop going along with the absurd notion that every federal function must be housed under one militarized Trump agency roof. | | ---|---|--- If Democrats are ever going to produce meaningful change, we should be willing to take real, structural action and not settle for symbolic reforms. This isn't a time for democratic party modesty and pragmatism. People are being rounded up and disappeared. Protesters have been shot and killed. And DHS still refuses to fully cooperate with local Minneapolis prosecutors. Defunding is not radical. It is the only language Donald Trump and his republican enablers in Congress will understand. Everything else is sermonizing--cheap, shallow, and utterly ineffective Politically and morally, democrats must be bold and strong. It's not enough to express shock at Trump's DHS, request technology changes, and ask federal agents to be more respectful of the constitution. It's time for democrats to defund DHS's enforcement arm and start over with new agencies and institutions that respect both the law and human rights. Enough already. name: Mark Kelly
subject: My day in court
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO**
body:
| | |
---
[](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3742?refcode=jtk359-bu-nd&t=1&akid=7343%2E400544%2EGBoqQf)
Team, it's Mark.

Most of you probably know that I am suing the Secretary of Defense. Last week, I was in court to make my case against Pete Hegseth.
After 25 years in uniform and another five in the United States Senate, I never imagined I’d have to do something like that. Hegseth has left me no choice.
When he censured me and threatened my rank and retirement, he came after what I earned over 25 years — and chilled the free speech rights of every retired member of the military alongside me.
If Hegseth isn’t stopped, our longest-serving military veterans will have to live with the constant threat that they could be deprived of their rank and pension years — even decades — after they leave the military if they say something he doesn’t like.
I’m not going to sit by and let that happen. So, I sued Hegseth. And now, we wait and see what the court decides.
I’ve been truly humbled by the support people have sent my way over the past couple of months. It means the world to me that so many of you care about what Trump and Hegseth are trying to do.
That’s how we’ve made it this far, and it’s how we’ll get to the other side of this chapter in our nation’s history. Together.
If you’ve been waiting for the right time to make a stand and join the fight, this is it.
**[Please, if you can, will you split a donation between Deb Haaland and me?](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/3742?refcode=jtk359-bu-nd&t=2&akid=7343%2E400544%2EGBoqQf)**

name: Deb Haaland
subject: This is who I am, what I stand for, and what I'll do on day one as governor
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO**
body:
There are two important things you should know about me...

| | | [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/4966?t=1&akid=7310%2E400544%2EnML-aq)
---
Friend — there are two important things you should know about me:

1\. Coming from 35 generations of Laguna Pueblo women, **I am both of and for New Mexico.** I have never forgotten my roots, and I never will. And I will _always_ fight for our communities.

2\. **I'm not afraid to stand up to Donald Trump** — or anyone else who threatens New Mexican communities and our way of life. I have _many_ times before, and __**_[that's what I'll do from day one as our governor, but I need your help to get there.](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/4966?t=2&akid=7310%2E400544%2EnML-aq)_**
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [Donate $10](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/4967?t=3&akid=7310%2E400544%2EnML-aq)
---
| | [Donate $35](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/4968?t=4&akid=7310%2E400544%2EnML-aq)
---
| | [Donate $50](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/4969?t=5&akid=7310%2E400544%2EnML-aq)
---
| | [Other Amount](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/4966?t=6&akid=7310%2E400544%2EnML-aq)
---
Like so many of you, I am furious and heartbroken at what is happening across our country right now. We must continue to fight back, in every way we can. Every one of us has the ability to speak up and to vote out those who are enabling the cruel policies of ICE and the Trump administration. Hope is only lost when resistance stops, and there is real power in our voices and our collective action.

That's why I've joined protesters in Albuquerque and Santa Fe, and it's why I will continue to stand in solidarity with all who oppose injustice, encourage our lawmakers to vote against additional DHS funding, and support efforts to require ICE agents to identify themselves, to remove their masks, and to ban ICE detention centers in our state.
Under-trained ICE agents should not be brutalizing communities, tearing families apart, or breaking the law. Lawful protesters should never be met with gunfire in the streets. This isn't just unacceptable — it's un-American.

**_Instead of destroying kids' lives, families, and futures, o_**** _ur country should be protecting them_**** _. That is what I will do from day one as our next governor._**
**You have my promise: I will hold Donald Trump accountable and protect New Mexicans _always._ If his administration wants to bring violence and chaos to our state, gut the healthcare and food assistance programs our families rely on, and decimate our public lands, they will have to go through me first.**
While this administration attacks our communities and hands out tax breaks to billionaires, I will fight for an education that gives every child the chance to dream big and reach their full potential here in New Mexico. I'll give families a raise by increasing the minimum wage and the child tax credit. I'll protect affordable healthcare and expand access to treatment and recovery programs for those suffering from addiction.
**But with New Mexico's history of switching parties every eight years and Republicans spending whatever it takes to buy the governor's office, flip our state red, and bring an extremist agenda,****I need you with me to deliver on these promises.
**
**[Please, will you chip in $5, or whatever you can comfortably spare, to help me become governor of New Mexico so I can stand up to Trump, keep our communities safe, and deliver real progress for our families?](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/4966?t=7&akid=7310%2E400544%2EnML-aq)**
| | [Donate](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/4966?t=8&akid=7310%2E400544%2EnML-aq)
---
Thank you so much.

Stay fierce,

Deb

clean_body: There are two important things you should know about me... | | | Friend — there are two important things you should know about me: 1\. Coming from 35 generations of Laguna Pueblo women, I am both of and for New Mexico. I have never forgotten my roots, and I never will. And I will always fight for our communities. 2\. I'm not afraid to stand up to Donald Trump — or anyone else who threatens New Mexican communities and our way of life. I have many times before, and _ If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | Like so many of you, I am furious and heartbroken at what is happening across our country right now. We must continue to fight back, in every way we can. Every one of us has the ability to speak up and to vote out those who are enabling the cruel policies of ICE and the Trump administration. Hope is only lost when resistance stops, and there is real power in our voices and our collective action. That's why I've joined protesters in Albuquerque and Santa Fe, and it's why I will continue to stand in solidarity with all who oppose injustice, encourage our lawmakers to vote against additional DHS funding, and support efforts to require ICE agents to identify themselves, to remove their masks, and to ban ICE detention centers in our state. Under-trained ICE agents should not be brutalizing communities, tearing families apart, or breaking the law. Lawful protesters should never be met with gunfire in the streets. This isn't just unacceptable — it's un-American. Instead of destroying kids' lives, families, and futures, o ur country should be protecting them . That is what I will do from day one as our next governor. You have my promise: I will hold Donald Trump accountable and protect New Mexicans always. If his administration wants to bring violence and chaos to our state, gut the healthcare and food assistance programs our families rely on, and decimate our public lands, they will have to go through me first. While this administration attacks our communities and hands out tax breaks to billionaires, I will fight for an education that gives every child the chance to dream big and reach their full potential here in New Mexico. I'll give families a raise by increasing the minimum wage and the child tax credit. I'll protect affordable healthcare and expand access to treatment and recovery programs for those suffering from addiction. But with New Mexico's history of switching parties every eight years and Republicans spending whatever it takes to buy the governor's office, flip our state red, and bring an extremist agenda,I need you with me to deliver on these promises. | | Thank you so much. Stay fierce, Deb | | | | |

name: Justin Streiff, Turning Point USA
subject: The book Charlie left behind
body:
[unsubscribe](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?xul=65a6x69jow166jo1opkmvmi0w7cnzxbvfszi5ychot9furgfiefig8kjm3ms149ymhc5e4hbzwtmfja38erv7tcw783vev8fx&__stmp=ta5hts&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh)
Please read the message below from our sponsor.
* * *
| | | [](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=ta5hts&__onlt=h)
---
| | Friend,
---
| | Charlie would have wanted you to have this.

Before his September 10th assassination, Charlie had just put the finishing touches on a very important book. His final book.

The topic is one that Charlie found to be crucial for every human to understand and practice.

This isn’t a book about public policy or political discourse, nor is it simply about passing on values of patriotism to young Americans.

**Instead, this book is about something far deeper and more personal — the importance of honoring the Sabbath and resting in God’s grace.**

Despite his hectic schedule and full life, Charlie was committed to setting aside an entire day each week to rest, to pray, and to give undivided attention to God, Erika, and his children.

By honoring the Sabbath, Charlie lived out a powerful message: _**“God, You are enough. And because You are enough, I need not carry the weight of a world that was never mine to hold.”**_

His book, _**Stop, in the Name of God**_ is the fruit of that conviction.
---
[](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=ta5hts&__onlt=h)
---
When you read it, you will hear Charlie’s voice inviting you to reclaim something our fast-paced culture has essentially erased: a time for rest, a time devoted to God, and time with family.

Charlie’s hope was for each of us to rediscover the Sabbath as he did: not as a burden to bear, but as a gift to receive...

A sacred routine meant to draw us nearer to God, to steady our hearts with His peace, and renew our strength for the battles to which He calls us.

I believe you will find this book inspiring.

Charlie wanted everyone to have this book because its message meant so much in his own life.

**[To claim your copy of this life-changing book, simply make a generous gift in any amount to Turning Point USA today.](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=ta5hts&__onlt=h)**
---
[ **Support Turning Point USA**
**& Receive Charlie’s Final Book**](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=ta5hts&__onlt=h)
---
In the months since Charlie’s assassination, there has been a massive surge of interest in Turning Point USA’s message among young Americans...

...and it’s not just the conservative message as a whole — _**it’s the message of FAITH!**_

In recent months, I have heard countless stories of people who have answered Charlie’s call to attend a Bible study, start a family, and pray to God.

Charlie devoted so much of his time urging people to build strong families, to go to church, and to stand up for America. _And it’s happening._

After years of witnessing declining church attendance, we are now seeing Americans filling the pews in huge numbers across the country.

What we’re witnessing is not just growth —**it’s revival.**

At Turning Point USA, we are dedicated and honored to continue strengthening the faith of Americans, young and old.

We are building a coalition of truth-preaching churches that stand firm against cultural compromise, defend biblical values, and strengthen families.

We are training pastors, uniting congregations, and, of course, reaching the next generation on campuses across the country with this same message.

You might feel disheartened by the evil in our culture and by the unthinkable assassination of Charlie.

**But America’s faithful are waking up. The youth are waking up. America is waking up.**

All across this nation, pastors are linking arms, young believers are finding their voice, and families are saying, _“We’re not backing down.”_

This is what Charlie prayed for.

I see this book as one of Charlie’s most enduring gifts to the world, and I know you will too.

None of us knew how brief his time on earth would be. But the truths written in this book are timeless. They will outlive us all, and most importantly, **_so will the legacy of Charlie’s faith._**

Charlie lived life at a relentless pace, but he also understood the sacred value of stillness. Through it all, his gaze was fixed on eternity with God.

**[To claim your copy of Charlie’s final book, simply make a gift, in any amount, to support Turning Point USA today.](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=ta5hts&__onlt=h)**
---
[ **Support Turning Point USA**
**& Receive Charlie’s Final Book**](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=ta5hts&__onlt=h)
---
I’ll personally ensure your copy is sent to you as soon as possible.

Thank you in advance for carrying on Charlie’s mission and supporting his legacy.
---
**For Charlie,**

**Justin Streiff**
Chief Operating Officer
Turning Point USA
---
[](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=ta5hts&__onlt=h)
---
**[Support Turning Point USA](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=ta5hts&__onlt=h)**
---
**P.S.** When you read **_Stop, in the Name of God,_** you will discover what Charlie found in the Sabbath: a holy rest that steadies the heart, renews the soul, and gives strength for the road ahead.

**[I hope you’ll claim your copy by making your most generous gift to Turning Point USA. Your support fuels our work to build the most faithful generation of Americans in history. Thank you, and God bless!](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=ta5hts&__onlt=h)**
---
[ **Support Turning Point USA**
**& Receive Charlie’s Final Book**](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=ta5hts&__onlt=h)
---
4940 East Beverly Road
Phoenix, Arizona 85044
---
[Unsubscribe](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?xul=65a6x69jow166jo1opkmvmi0w7cnzxbvfszi5ychot9furgfiefig8kjm3ms149ymhc5e4hbzwtmfja38erv7tcw783vev8fx&__stmp=ta5hts&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh) / [Privacy Policy](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?xtl=11qfvsq9v6woyyndvpgbcmsxf0vdsvcbl1o4jvbg0i19717jkz7ptuqhed0h7ov3abdkcg1pbw0gaesd4xfl2qv6rn9r8yga649xe6g378k1konlsrzw5pdpem5y&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=ta5hts&__onlt=h) / [Contact us](https://trk.thedcbugle.com/?xtl=ukxuchlat53hez1dbmmrcna91lhtfn4bhxpg13revdb774yrl6km133zab03djq13q30c5gv8jglqepdswrzfcn2zrqj8m9sjb8y9zptk93icvn3f8v1n&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=ta5hts&__onlt=h)

Y’all, it’s Jasmine. I’ll get straight to it. There are three reasons I’m asking you to support my Senate race right now:
**1\. Texas is more competitive than ever.**
Republicans won’t say it out loud — but they know they’re about to lose Texas. SIX Texas GOP incumbents have announced they’re quitting. They see the same thing we do: this state is changing, and their grip is slipping.
**2\. I’m the candidate they fear most.**
Every time I’ve stood up to Donald Trump, his attacks have backfired. They know I don’t flinch. They know I don’t fold. _And they know I will bring that same fire to the U.S. Senate._
**3\. We have a real path to victory — but only if we show early strength.**
Our internal polling shows a clear opening... but also a clear warning: _if we fall behind early, Republicans will bury this race in dark money._
[**That’s why I’m asking personally: Will you chip in $5, $10, or anything you can to help us build momentum right now?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/124985399/600323479/-1529437191?amount=10&refcode=260208__E1A3_JC_ThreeReasonsRS&refcode2=13f06d94-1305-f111-832f-000d3a1f0e4c&contactdata=Ys1lLixw5U2G7NQEgPpYfP8TXsgbrlVGD2bjmWOLSCYGIPzDd3vBpsjpzSqHiqiZ&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDMwOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxM2YwNmQ5NC0xMzA1LWYxMTEtODMyZi0wMDBkM2ExZjBlNGMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8u7L6KvKbMLPgWZs1ZCPVlJQuXfmjtiywcR1MAbTeW8=)

clean_body: = | | | = Y’all, it’s Jasmine. I’ll get straight to it. There are three reasons I’m asking you to support my Senate race right now: 1\. Texas is more competitive than ever. Republicans won’t say it out loud — but they know they’re about to lose Texas. SIX Texas GOP incumbents have announced they’re quitting. They see the same thing we do: this state is changing, and their grip is slipping. 2\. I’m the candidate they fear most. Every time I’ve stood up to Donald Trump, his attacks have backfired. They know I don’t flinch. They know I don’t fold. And they know I will bring that same fire to the U.S. Senate. 3\. We have a real path to victory — but only if we show early strength. clean_body: Governors have never mattered more than they do at this moment. | Team, I am honored to serve as Vice Chair of the Democratic Governors Association — and it’s a responsibility I take seriously. Because governors have never mattered more than they do at this moment, and this year 36 governor’s races will be decided. While Washington Republicans cater to megadonors and fuel chaos, Democratic governors are on the front lines — protecting fundamental freedoms, lowering costs, and delivering real results for working families. We have 36 chances to stop far-right extremism at the state level, 36 opportunities to elect people who actually want to solve problems, and 36 races that will shape the future of this country. Your support matters in all 36 of these races. It helps campaigns organize, fight back against Republican attacks, educate and engage with voters, and so much more. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Thank you for being part of this fight. — Gretchen Fight Like Hell PAC PO Box 15093 Lansing, MI 48901 United States ** | mailto:info@fightlikehellpac.org| | Paid for by Fight Like Hell PAC. I promised fellow Republican leaders a response from Peter.

_And I would NEVER not follow through on a promise. _

From when I took an oath to our country as a Marine to my swearing in as Vice President, I take my word seriously.

**[I don’t want to have to show up to my meeting empty-handed. There's a lot of people in the swamp who like to make empty promises.

**_I 'm proud to say that I'm not one of them. _**

When I say something, I mean it. So when I promised my fellow Republicans I'd have your responses ready, I was ready to go to any length to get them.
